Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for BrightLoop-related threads using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop" or "Tessa Moreno" or "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb"). Find Mina's account update to David covering two active April relocations for BrightLoop. Find the Road Runner Auto Transport dispatch delay notice sent to Blessing on April 11 about Marcus Webb's vehicle shipment. Find Marcus Webb's follow-up emails asking about his car delivery status with no response. Find Chloe's operational gaps email identifying two specific problems: Blessing missed the Road Runner delay notice for Marcus Webb's vehicle, and Simone Richter's relocation was marked as a 1BR but she ended up in a studio. Find Mina's factual reconstruction pulling the full picture together. Search for any emails from tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com raising concerns about either failure. Discovery: Tessa Moreno has not yet contacted MoveOps about the service issues directly.
Search Slack for BrightLoop and temp coordinator discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "BrightLoop" or "temp coordinator" or "capacity" or "Simone" or "Marcus Webb"). Find Chloe's and David's April 24-25 messages in #executive raising capacity concerns and requesting a temporary coordinator from Elena. Find David's post explaining that BrightLoop recovery work is inseparable from May staffing. Find Chloe's end-of-day April 24 message noting that Elena still has not responded to the temp coordinator request. Read full thread context using conversations_replies where needed.
Search CRM for BrightLoop using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop"). Find the company record. Search for deals using crm_search_deals. Find the April 2026 deal covering Simone Richter and Marcus Webb relocations at $11,350. Check engagement history using crm_list_engagements to find detailed notes on both relocations including Simone's rush 5-day turnaround with temp housing and Marcus's vehicle shipping add-on through Road Runner.
Search Airtable for BrightLoop relocation records by listing bases with airtable_list_bases and tables with airtable_list_tables, then searching with airtable_search_records (query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb"). Find Simone Richter's record (Chicago to Boston, April 4-6, In Progress, rush, 2 weeks furnished temp housing) and Marcus Webb's record (Atlanta to Boston, April 14-18, In Progress, vehicle shipping for 2019 Honda Civic via Road Runner Auto Transport).
Search Linear for BrightLoop-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone" or "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner"). Find any existing tickets tracking the service failures and recovery work. Read comments using linear_list_comments for additional context on the operational gaps.
Search emails for Terraform Digital communications using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "Catalina" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "Mina Hashimoto"). Find the reply-all incident details and Catalina's apology email to Rachel Nguyen. Find Rachel Nguyen's April 22 email to Julian Brooks requesting three employee relocations: Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, move-in May 5), Marcus Liu (Portland to New York, move-in May 12), and Samantha Obi (Denver to San Francisco, move-in May 8). Find Julian's forward to David flagging the opportunity. Find Rachel's response to the accidental reply-all pricing thread asking who her actual account manager is. Read full threads using get_thread for context on whether Mina has since taken over communications or if onboarding is stalled.
Search Slack for Terraform discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "Terraform" or "Catalina pricing" or "Mina"). Find Elena's April 22 directive in #executive assigning Mina as account owner, authorizing a dedicated Terraform coordinator hire, ordering Catalina to send a personal apology, and directing the team to honor the pricing Rachel saw. Find David's follow-up confirming Mina is fully responsible for the account going forward.
Search CRM for Terraform Digital using crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform" or "Vantage Distributed"). Find the company record. Search deals using crm_search_deals. Find the Q2 2026 Relocation Batch 1 deal at $7,500 in qualification stage covering three employees. Check engagement history using crm_list_engagements for the reply-all incident log and the account handoff from Catalina to Mina.
Search Airtable for Terraform relocation records using airtable_search_records (query: "Terraform" or "Priya Dasgupta" or "Marcus Liu" or "Samantha Obi"). Check whether any relocation records have been created for the three employees. This reveals whether Mina has actually set up the operational side or if onboarding is stalled.
Search emails for the Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice situation using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "Raj Greenfield" or "INV-2026-0398" or "invoice swap"). Find Hana's April 10 invoice emails to Raj Greenfield at Sunbelt and Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto. Find Raj's initial question to Emeka about the wrong PDF showing a Palmetto Community Foundation header and a $2,400 total instead of the expected Sunbelt amount. Find Raj's follow-up and his separate escalation email to David. Find Victoria Chen-Nakamura's email to David asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies. Find Carmen Delgado-Reyes' emails flagging that the $4,800 Sunbelt invoice does not match Palmetto's nonprofit rate and requesting a corrected explanation in writing before her May 15 audit deadline. Find Marcus Thorne's analysis treating this as a pricing exposure and compliance issue. Find David's framing email connecting this to the Terraform reply-all as a repeat pricing-exposure event. Find Emeka's response to Raj explaining the document-send error. Read full threads using get_thread.
Search CRM for Sunbelt and Palmetto using crm_search_companies and crm_search_deals. Find the Sunbelt April 2026 deal at $4,800 (INV-2026-0398) and Palmetto April 2026 deal at $2,400 (INV-2026-0399). Confirm that the underlying billing records are correct but the PDFs sent on April 10 were swapped between the two clients.
Search QuickBooks for the Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to verify the actual amounts. Confirm INV-2026-0398 for Sunbelt at $4,800 (commercial rate) and INV-2026-0399 for Palmetto at $2,400 (nonprofit rate). The accounting is correct; what failed was the outbound email attachment layer.
Search Linear for any existing ticket tracking the invoice swap situation using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice"). Find ticket #417 titled "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation," assigned to Marcus Thorne, created April 15. Since a tracking ticket already exists, there is no need to create a new one.
Search emails for the expense auto-categorizer status using search_emails (query: "ExpenseBot" or "expense auto" or "auto-categorizer" or "pilot"). Find Elena's March 17 directive pausing auto-approve and auto-deny while allowing auto-categorization to continue with human review. Find Samira's March 18 confirmation that auto-decisions have been disabled for both pilot clients (Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics). Find the completed audit results covering 47 expenses: 39 correct at 83% accuracy, 5 false rejections from Vectral, and 3 false approvals from Mosaic totaling $885 in financial exposure that MoveOps absorbed per Elena's directive.
Search Slack for expense-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "auto-approve" or "pilot"). Find Lena's confirmation in #engineering that auto-approval and auto-denial have been turned off and configurations updated. Find Samira's pilot monitoring updates.
Search Linear for expense-related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or "ExpenseBot" or "pilot"). Find the pilot monitoring ticket (ENG-211) and related remediation tickets. Read comments using linear_list_comments on ENG-211 to find Samira's confirmation that auto_decisions_enabled is set to false as of March 18. Find the remediation tickets: ENG-216 (audit), ENG-217 (pipeline fix), ENG-218 (dashboard types), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist), ENG-212 (schema validation tests). Check their statuses and the earliest possible re-enablement target.
Search Airtable stipend records for flagged transactions using airtable_search_records (query: "flagged" or "Pending" or "duplicate" or specific employee names). Find the duplicate receipt pair: Kevin Tran at Vectral ($89 ergonomic keyboard) and Andre Washington at Mosaic ($89 ergonomic keyboard) sharing an identical SHA-256 receipt hash, with QuickBooks holds placed on both. Find Jordan Ekwueme at BrightLoop ($45 monitor stand) flagged for matching PDF template metadata. Find Andre Washington's incorrectly auto-approved $1,200 standing desk (over the $800 policy cap) and $350 coworking membership (excluded category under Mosaic policy).
Look up Tessa Moreno's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tessa Moreno"). Find tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com.
Look up David Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find david.chen@moveops.com.
Look up Samira Tariq's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq"). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com.
Find the #executive Slack channel by listing channels using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Identify channel C004 as the executive channel.
Send an email to Tessa Moreno using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com], cc: [david.chen@moveops.com]). Subject should reference BrightLoop coordinator support. Body should confirm that the temporary coordinator is authorized, that MoveOps is adding capacity to support BrightLoop's account going forward, and convey that Elena is personally ensuring the account gets the attention it needs.
Create a CRM engagement for BrightLoop using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [BrightLoop company ID]). Body should document the current account status including the two April service failures (Simone Richter housing mismatch where a studio was delivered instead of the promised 1BR, and Marcus Webb vehicle delay where the Road Runner delay notice on April 11 went unactioned), recovery actions in progress, the temp coordinator authorization, and the upcoming May expansion with six new Boston relocations.
Create a CRM engagement for Terraform Digital using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [Terraform company ID]). Body should document the account handoff from Catalina Dubois to Mina Hashimoto after the April 22 reply-all pricing leak, the current onboarding status for the Q2 Batch 1 relocations covering Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, and Samantha Obi, and any gaps or outstanding items discovered during the investigation.
Post a summary in the #executive Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C004). The message should cover all four investigation areas: BrightLoop service failures and temp coordinator status, Terraform onboarding status after the AM swap, Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice exposure including client reactions from Raj Greenfield and Carmen Delgado-Reyes, and the expense auto-categorizer engineering status. This gives the leadership team full visibility into everything Elena asked about.
Check Elena's calendar for early next week availability using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: 2026-04-27, end_datetime: 2026-04-29) to find an open slot. Then schedule the check-in using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: something like "Samira check-in, expense tool timeline", start_datetime: an available slot on April 27 or 28, attendees: [elena.rostova@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com]). The meeting is for Elena to get a proper update on the expense auto-categorizer timeline and next steps.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Create a CRM engagement for Terraform Digital using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [Terraform company ID]). Body should document the account handoff from Catalina Dubois to Mina Hashimoto after the April 22 reply-all pricing leak, the current onboarding status for the Q2 Batch 1 relocations covering Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, and Samantha Obi, and any gaps or outstanding items discovered during the investigation.
Post a summary in the #executive Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C004). The message should cover all four investigation areas: BrightLoop service failures and temp coordinator status, Terraform onboarding status after the AM swap, Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice exposure including client reactions from Raj Greenfield and Carmen Delgado-Reyes, and the expense auto-categorizer engineering status. This gives the leadership team full visibility into everything Elena asked about.
Check Elena's calendar for early next week availability using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: 2026-04-27, end_datetime: 2026-04-29) to find an open slot. Then schedule the check-in using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: something like "Samira check-in, expense tool timeline", start_datetime: an available slot on April 27 or 28, attendees: [elena.rostova@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com]). The meeting is for Elena to get a proper update on the expense auto-categorizer timeline and next steps.

outcome

✗Run #1The final response says 'Good news: It's not stalled. Mina moved immediately after you assigned her. Rachel Nguyen responded the next morning with full details for all 3 employees.' The agent found the opposite of what the criterion expects — it reported onboarding is progressing, not stalled. ✗Run #2The final response characterizes the Terraform situation as 'Actually Recovering' and states Mina 'picked up the thread after the Catalina swap and is executing well.' The correct finding — that onboarding is operationally stalled despite email communications — was not identified.

—Run #4No justification
✗Run #5The final response states 'The onboarding is progressing' for Terraform Digital, contradicting the correct finding that onboarding is operationally stalled with no Airtable relocation records created. ✓Run #6The final response states 'zero relocation records in Airtable, the $6,560 estimate hasn't been converted to an invoice, no coordinator assigned, CRM deal stuck at qualification, and the coordinator hire you authorized has never been posted' — clearly identifying the Terraform onboarding as stalled after the AM swap.
